programme.title: PL STORIES
programme.desc: We look back on the extraordinary career of Mohamed Salah, tracing his rise from a small village in Egypt to global football stardom. programme.title: PL STORIES
programme.desc: We look back on the extraordinary career of Mohamed Salah, tracing his rise from a small village in Egypt to global football stardom. We speak to those closest to him to highlight his early years, struggles, and determination, shedding light on the pivotal moments that shaped his journey to becoming a Premier League legend with Liverpool.

programme.title: PL STORIES
programme.desc: We dive into the fiercest rivalry in English football-Manchester United and Liverpool. As Liverpool draw level with United's record haul of top-flight titles, this is a story of history, pride, and the relentless pursuit of greatness. With insight from icons on both sides-Ian Rush, Lou Macari, Brian Kidd, Michael Owen, Phil Thompson, and new Liverpool boss Arne Slot, we unpack the moments that have defined generations, split families, and lit up the Premier League.

programme.title: PL STORIES
programme.desc: We follow New Zealand striker Chris Wood during a crucial World Cup Qualifier on home soil, at the peak of one of the most impressive seasons of his career. Back in his hometown, we meet the family, coaches and friends who helped shape the player and the person he's become. From humble beginnings to Premier League mainstay, Wood reflects on the pressures he's faced, the pride of leading his country, and the mindset that's driven him every step of the way.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW
programme.desc: With the remnants of the celebratory confetti barely gone from the Anfield pitch, Curtis Jones joins the Big Interview to reflect on an historic title-winning season. Having signed for his beloved team at just 9 years of age, the Liverpool born and bred midfielder explains just how much the title triumph means to him and sheds light on the impact of Arne Slot and the differences in approach of his new manager compared to Jurgen Klopp.

programme.title: PL MASTERCLASS
programme.desc: Some of the Premier League's biggest stars take us onto the pitch to discuss the techniques and thought processes that allow them to perform at the highest level. Featuring Jan Paul Van Hecke, Leicester City and Brentford goalscorers and West Ham's Thomas Soucek.

programme.title: End of Transmission
programme.desc: End of Transmission.

programme.title: PL Stories 2024/25
programme.desc: As the curtain comes down on Everton's time at Goodison Park, PL Stories takes a poignant look back at one of English football's most iconic grounds. For over 130 years, Goodison has been more than just a stadium; it's been a home, a fortress, and the stage for countless moments of triumph, heartbreak, and unforgettable drama.

programme.title: The Big Interview 2024/25
programme.desc: Beautiful! was the reaction from Morato when he first set eyes on The City Ground. Having joined Nottingham Forest last summer, the Brazilian sits down with The Big Interview in the final week of what's been a remarkable season. The 23-year-old shares his thoughts about Forest's fortunes under Nuno Espirito Santo, his career journey to England and how losing his sister to cancer when she was just 18 has impacted his life.

programme.title: PL GOATs 2024/25
programme.desc: Premier League legend Tim Howard takes on the ultimate challenge, ranking the 10 greatest US players to play in the Premier League. But there's a twist; from his carefully selected list, he must crown one as the Ultimate Premier League USA GOAT.

programme.title: Everton vs Southampton (MW37)
programme.desc: This will be Everton's 2,789th and final game at Goodison Park in all competitions (W1537 D660 L591). They're currently winless in seven home games (D5 L2), last having a longer run between October and December 2009 (8).

programme.title: Ipswich Town vs West Ham United (MW38)
programme.desc: West Ham have won just one of their last 10 Premier League games (D4 L5), though it was in their last away match against Manchester United. The Hammers last won consecutive away league games in December 2023.

programme.title: AFC Bournemouth vs Leicester City (MW38)
programme.desc: Bournemouth are unbeaten in all six of their Premier League home games against Leicester (W4 D2), winning the last three by an aggregate score of 10-4.

programme.title: Nottingham Forest vs Chelsea (MW38)
programme.desc: The 33rd season of the Premier League sees Manchester City defend its title against a host of teams keen for glory themselves. 20 teams are in the mix but only 1 champion will emerge ultimately.

programme.title: Fulham vs Manchester City (MW38)
programme.desc: Fulham are winless in 19 Premier League meetings with Man City (D3 L16), losing the last 14 in a row. Their last victory was in April 2009 at the Etihad Stadium under Roy Hodgson.

programme.title: Newcastle United vs Everton (MW38)
programme.desc: Victory for Newcastle will guarantee them qualification for next season's UEFA Champions League, the second time in the last three seasons they'll have done so having only done so twice previously (1997-98 and 2002-03).

programme.title: Tottenham Hotspur vs Brighton and Hove Albion (MW38)
programme.desc: Although Spurs are 17th in the Premier League, they've scored more goals (63) than they've conceded (61). The lowest position in a Premier League table a side has finished with a positive goal difference is 16th, by Man City in 2003-04 (+1).

programme.title: Southampton vs Arsenal (MW38)
programme.desc: Southampton have lost 29 Premier League games this season. Defeat here would see them become the first team to lose 30 times in a single campaign in the competition's history, while also the first team to do so in a 38-game top-flight season.

programme.title: PL Stories 2024/25
programme.desc: As the curtain comes down on Everton's time at Goodison Park, PL Stories takes a poignant look back at one of English football's most iconic grounds. For over 130 years, Goodison has been more than just a stadium; it's been a home, a fortress, and the stage for countless moments of triumph, heartbreak, and unforgettable drama.

programme.title: The Big Interview 2024/25
programme.desc: Beautiful! was the reaction from Morato when he first set eyes on The City Ground. Having joined Nottingham Forest last summer, the Brazilian sits down with The Big Interview in the final week of what's been a remarkable season. The 23-year-old shares his thoughts about Forest's fortunes under Nuno Espirito Santo, his career journey to England and how losing his sister to cancer when she was just 18 has impacted his life.

programme.title: Liverpool vs Crystal Palace (MW38)
programme.desc: When finishing the season at home, the Premier League champions have won their final match on 13 out of the last 14 occasions. The only exception was Manchester United losing against West Ham in 2006-07.

programme.title: Manchester United vs Aston Villa (MW38)
programme.desc: Manchester United will finish this Premier League campaign without winning two matches in a row all season - it's only the third time in the club's history that they've gone an entire league season without winning back-to-back games.

programme.title: Wolverhampton Wanderers vs Brentford (MW38)
programme.desc: Brentford have scored 65 Premier League goals this season, their most in a top-flight campaign since 1937-38 (69). Their conversion rate in 2024-25 is 15.2%, the best of any side.

programme.title: Ipswich Town vs West Ham United (MW38)
programme.desc: West Ham have won just one of their last 10 Premier League games (D4 L5), though it was in their last away match against Manchester United. The Hammers last won consecutive away league games in December 2023.

programme.desc: Bournemouth are unbeaten in all six of their Premier League home games against Leicester (W4 D2), winning the last three by an aggregate score of 10-4.

programme.desc: Although Spurs are 17th in the Premier League, they've scored more goals (63) than they've conceded (61). The lowest position in a Premier League table a side has finished with a positive goal difference is 16th, by Man City in 2003-04 (+1).

programme.title: Nottingham Forest vs Chelsea (MW38)
programme.desc: Nottingham Forest have won 19 Premier League matches this season, more than they did in 2022-23 and 2023-24 combined (18). They last won 20+ games in 1994-95 (22), while they've never done so in a 38-game top-flight season.
